inline_comments.message: Some comments here would help.  At a minimum, list what parameters the function is expecting.

I've done some testing in my environment, and it appears that dmaap-bc will reject an attempt to create a new publisher that has the same username and password, even if the location is different.  I believe the test for locations being equal should be dropped.  (To put it somewhat differently, we should reuse an existing publisher if the username and password are the same.)

I did not test whether dmaap-bc will reject a request to create a subscriber if the username, password, and deliveryURL match and the dcaeLocation is different.  I suspect it might.  It might also reject a request if just the username and password match.  Please check this.

There is difference in behaviour of DR Publisher & Subscriber creation.
While creating a new publisher dmaap-bc will reject an attempt to create publisher if the location is different but the same is not true for subscriber, dmaap-bc creates a new subscriber if the username/password is same and dcaeLocationName is different.

Tested for both cases:
1. UserName/Password & deliveryURL matches with different dcaeLocationName dmaap-bc creates a new subscriber.
2. UserName/Password & dcaeLocationName matches with different deliveryURL dmaap-bc creates a new subscriber.

To make sure we resue any entity of subscriber having similar userName/password/deliveryURL/dcaeLocationName checks are in place in script.
For the publisher case, then, shouldn't the code omit the test for "${dr_loc}" = "${_dr_loc}"?

Yeah,can be updated but skipping check for dcaeLocationName and wait for Dmaap to fail creation of dr publisher REST call later due to similar location, its better to check and stop the REST call which is going to fail at later point of time.

Maybe I'm not seeing this correctly.  

Suppose I've already created a publisher for feed F with username jack, password xyz, and location chicago.   Then, later, in another deployment, I try to add a publisher for feed F with username jack, password xyz, and location dallas.  The code you have here would not treat the new publisher as a duplicate (because the location is not the same), so it would make a call to dmaap-bc to create a publisher with username jack, password xyz, and location dallas.  But dmaap-bc considers this a duplicate and will fail.

I tried this in November after building a dbc-client container from this code, and that's the result I saw.
Hi Jack, I understood the concern here, thanks for pointing it out, now removed check "${dr_loc}" = "${_dr_loc}" for publisher, please have a check if it looks fine.
